description: Update the Rivet frontend dashboard styling to match the Foundry (sandbox-agent) frontend styling. Color/theme-only migration with no layout or component structure changes. The Rivet architecture (Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, CSS variables, CVA) stays intact.

userStories.title: Take before screenshots of all main screens
userStories.description: As a developer, I need before screenshots of every main dashboard screen so I can compare them after the theme migration.
acceptanceCriteria: Start the Rivet engine (cargo run or equivalent, serving API on port 6420)
Start the frontend dev server: cd frontend && pnpm dev:engine (port 43708, served at http://localhost:43708/ui)
Use agent-browser (dev-browser skill) to navigate to each screen and take screenshots
Capture: Actors dashboard (list + detail panel), Namespace settings, Deployments page, Logs viewer, Metrics dashboard, Connect page, sidebar navigation
Save all screenshots to .agent/screenshots/before/ with descriptive filenames
At least 6 distinct screenshots captured successfully
userStories.notes: The engine UI is at http://localhost:43708/ui. The frontend proxies /api to localhost:6420. Docker infra (postgres, nats, clickhouse) should already be running via docker/dev. If auth is required, the engine mode should work without Clerk.

userStories.title: Update dark mode CSS variables to Foundry zinc palette
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the dark mode CSS variables updated from warm brown-tinted colors to Foundry's cool zinc palette.
acceptanceCriteria: In frontend/packages/components/public/theme.css, update :root[class~='dark'] block with new values
userStories.notes: This is the canonical theme source. The border value '240 5% 11%' approximates Foundry's rgba(255,255,255,0.10) rendered on #09090b. This preserves existing Tailwind patterns that expect HSL values.

userStories.title: Update light mode CSS variables to Foundry zinc palette
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the light mode CSS variables updated to match Foundry's light theme using the zinc scale.
acceptanceCriteria: In frontend/packages/components/public/theme.css, update :root block with new values
userStories.notes: Light mode is defined in the :root block. Even though the dashboard is dark-mode-only (hardcoded class='dark'), keep light mode values aligned for consistency and future light mode support.

userStories.title: Mirror theme.css changes to src/components/theme.css
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the mirror copy of theme.css kept in sync with the canonical source.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/components/theme.css matches frontend/packages/components/public/theme.css exactly for all CSS variable definitions
Both dark and light mode variables are identical in both files
Chart color variables are identical in both files
userStories.notes: There are two copies of theme.css. The canonical source is packages/components/public/theme.css. The mirror is src/components/theme.css. Both must stay in sync.

userStories.title: Update chart colors to Foundry accent palette
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the chart color variables updated to use the Foundry accent-driven palette.
acceptanceCriteria: Light mode chart colors also updated to complement the new palette
userStories.notes: Chart colors are defined in separate :root and .dark blocks in theme.css. Update both.

userStories.title: Replace Open Sans with IBM Plex Sans font
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the sans-serif font changed from Open Sans to IBM Plex Sans to match Foundry's typography.
acceptanceCriteria: In frontend/packages/components/public/theme.css, replace Google Fonts @import URL to load IBM Plex Sans (weights 400,500,600,700) and IBM Plex Mono (weights 400,500) instead of Open Sans
In frontend/src/components/tailwind-base.ts, update fontFamily.sans from ['Open Sans', ...] to ['IBM Plex Sans', 'Segoe UI', 'ui-sans-serif', 'system-ui', 'sans-serif']
In frontend/src/components/tailwind-base.ts, update fontFamily['mono-console'] from ['Consolas', 'Lucida Console', ...] to ['IBM Plex Mono', 'SFMono-Regular', 'Consolas', 'monospace']
userStories.notes: IBM Plex Sans is a more technical, neutral font matching Foundry's design language. IBM Plex Mono replaces Consolas for code/console text.

userStories.title: Update React Flow dark mode overrides
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the React Flow styling overrides in index.css updated to use the new zinc palette instead of warm-tinted colors.
acceptanceCriteria: In frontend/src/index.css, update any hardcoded color values in the .dark .react-flow CSS rules
Control button backgrounds, borders, and text colors use zinc-based values instead of warm-tinted ones
Minimap background and border colors updated to match new card/border variables
Edge path colors reference the new muted-foreground values
userStories.notes: React Flow overrides in index.css use hardcoded color values that bypass CSS variables. These need manual updates to match the new zinc palette.

userStories.title: Scan and update hardcoded colors in components
userStories.description: As a developer, I need any hardcoded warm-tinted color values in component files updated to match the zinc palette.
acceptanceCriteria: Search frontend/src/ for hardcoded HSL values with warm hues (hue 12-34) and update to zinc equivalents
Search for hardcoded hex values that are warm-tinted grays and update to zinc equivalents
Update bg-amber-950/50 in warning alert components if it clashes with the new palette
Status indicator colors reviewed and updated if needed to align with Foundry's GitHub-inspired palette (success: #7ee787, error: #ffa198)
Verify Clerk theme in frontend/src/routes/__root.tsx still maps correctly (it uses CSS variables, so should auto-update)
userStories.notes: Most components use CSS variables via Tailwind classes, so they auto-update. This story catches any that bypass the variable system. Focus on grep for direct hsl(), #hex values, and Tailwind classes referencing specific warm colors.

userStories.title: Take after screenshots and save for comparison
userStories.description: As a developer, I need after screenshots of every main dashboard screen so I can compare them with the before screenshots.
acceptanceCriteria: Frontend dev server is running with the updated theme
Use agent-browser (dev-browser skill) to navigate to each screen and take screenshots
Capture the same screens as US-001: Actors dashboard, Namespace settings, Deployments, Logs, Metrics, Connect, sidebar
Save all screenshots to .agent/screenshots/after/ with the same filenames as before screenshots
At least 6 distinct screenshots captured successfully
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use identical navigation paths and screen names as US-001 so the before/after pairs are easy to compare side-by-side.
